title: Chiller Plant Piping
description: Assemble the primary-secondary chilled water piping loop in the correct order from the chiller evaporator through the building distribution and back.
explanation: Primary-secondary chilled water systems use a decoupler pipe to hydraulically separate the constant-flow primary loop (chiller protection) from the variable-flow secondary loop (building load). The decoupler must be between the two pumping systems so each loop operates independently. This arrangement follows ASHRAE Handbook — HVAC Systems and Equipment recommendations for central chiller plants.

title: Boiler Cascade System
description: Assemble the hot water boiler cascade piping from the lead boiler through the distribution system and back to the return header.
explanation: In a cascade boiler system, the lead boiler fires first and feeds a common header shared with lag boilers. The three-way mixing valve blends hot supply water with return water to deliver the correct supply temperature to the building loop, protecting condensing boilers from thermal shock and enabling outdoor air reset control per ASHRAE 90.1 requirements.

title: Cooling Tower Circuit
description: Assemble the condenser water circuit from the condenser water pump through the cooling tower and back to the chiller condenser bundle.
explanation: The condenser water circuit pumps warm water from the chiller condenser up to the cooling tower, where evaporative cooling rejects heat to atmosphere. Water collects in the basin, passes through a strainer to remove debris (protecting the chiller tubes), flows through the condenser bundle to absorb heat, then returns to the pump suction. The basin strainer is critical for preventing tube fouling per CTI and ASHRAE guidelines.

title: Steam Distribution System
description: Assemble the complete steam distribution path from the boiler through the process load and condensate return back to the boiler.
explanation: Steam systems distribute high-pressure steam from the boiler through a main header with drip legs to remove condensate. A PRV station reduces pressure for downstream equipment. After the steam gives up its latent heat at the process coil, the steam trap removes condensate while blocking live steam, directing it to a condensate receiver for return to the boiler. Proper trap selection and placement are critical per ASME and ASHRAE standards.

title: Chemical Feed System
description: Assemble the condenser water chemical treatment system from the chemical storage tank through the injection point and blowdown path.
explanation: Chemical treatment for condenser water systems starts at the storage tank, where a metering pump precisely doses chemicals through an injection quill inserted into the pipe. A sample port with corrosion coupon rack allows water quality monitoring. The blowdown valve controls cycles of concentration by discharging high-TDS water to a floor drain with an air gap to prevent cross-contamination, per ASHRAE Guideline 12 and local plumbing codes.

title: VFD-Driven Pump Assembly
description: Assemble the VFD-driven chilled water pump installation from the electrical disconnect through to the system header.
explanation: A VFD-driven pump assembly starts with a fused disconnect for lockout/tagout safety (NEC 430), then the VFD for variable speed control. The motor includes a shaft grounding ring to prevent bearing damage from VFD-induced stray currents. A flexible coupling between motor and pump absorbs misalignment and vibration. The check valve on the pump discharge prevents reverse flow when the pump is off. This sequence follows NEC, ASHRAE 90.1, and Hydraulic Institute standards.

title: Air Separator System
description: Assemble the hydronic air separation and expansion system in the correct order from the system pump through the building loop and return.
explanation: The air separator must be located at the point of lowest solubility — the pump discharge where water is at its highest temperature and lowest pressure relative to the system. The expansion tank connects at or near the air separator, establishing the system's point of no pressure change. This placement ensures maximum air removal efficiency and proper system pressurization per ASHRAE Handbook — HVAC Systems and Equipment.

title: Boiler Heat Recovery System
description: Assemble the boiler flue gas heat recovery system from the boiler exhaust through the feedwater preheat path and back to the boiler.
explanation: Boiler heat recovery captures waste heat from flue gases using an economizer coil, which preheats the feedwater before it enters the boiler. The deaerator removes dissolved oxygen and CO2 that cause corrosion, operating at elevated temperature from the recovered heat. The boiler feed pump then pressurizes the treated water above boiler operating pressure for injection. This arrangement can improve overall boiler plant efficiency by 5-10% per ASHRAE 90.1 and ASME guidelines.
